fault_hypothesis: Lines 73-74 incorrectly pop the last element when both streams share the same last value, but the docstring says both copies should be preserved. This post-processing removal is wrong and should not exist.

constraints.intent: After the pop, the merged list should still contain all elements from both inputs, but the pop removes one element incorrectly.
Inside the then-branch that pops an element, the count invariant is violated when both streams share their last value.

spec.expr: not (left and right and merged__1 and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1]) or len(merged__1) > len(left) + len(right)

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for valid inputs where both copies must be preserved.
The returned merged list must contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements.
